perturbation.original_text: Either Party, upon two (2) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement with respect to disclosures made thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party, upon five (5) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement with respect to disclosures made thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'two (2) days' to 'five (5) days' creates an in-text contradiction. The notice period for termination is ambiguous, leading to uncertainty in enforcement. The original article 6 being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective until terminated in accordance with this Article. Either Party, upon five (5) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement with respect to disclosures made thereafter. The rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder in respect of Information received under and subject to the Agreement shall survive such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for termination from 'two (2) days' to 'five (5) days'. The contradicted text states 'Either Party, upon five (5) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement'. Since the original text says two days and the changed text now says five days, it creates an inconsistency within the contract document itself, violating the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be modified or amended only by a written document duly executed by authorized representatives of Entera and .
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be modified or amended by either a written document duly executed by authorized representatives of Entera and or verbal confirmation.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'or verbal confirmation' creates an in-text contradiction. Changing the Agreement in writing or verbal is a point of contradiction here in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be modified or amended by either a written document duly executed by authorized representatives of Entera and or verbal confirmation. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have duly executed this Agreement as a sealed instrument, subject to and governed by the laws of the state of California this nd day of , 2000. ENTERA, INC. " " By: By: Name: Name: Title: Title:
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement can only be modified by a written document. The changed text introduces the possibility of modification by 'verbal confirmation'. The contradicted text is in the same location, and this change directly contradicts the requirement for written modification by also allowing verbal confirmation. This creates a clear inconsistency within the agreement, making it unclear how the agreement can be modified, thereby satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: f. to return or destroy Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: f. to return Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the option to 'destroy' creates an in-text contradiction regarding how the the reciever should act.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees: a. that with respect to Information, a confidential relationship is established between the Parties, and that Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above; b. to treat Information as confidential and to exercise no less care in its protection than Receiving Party uses in protecting its proprietary and confidential Information, or if Receiving Party has established no such standard of care, then Receiving Party shall exercise such care as the Disclosing Party observes to protect Information; <PAGE> c. to restrict dissemination of Information within its organization to employees having a need to know in connection with the purpose of the disclosure set forth above, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the proprietary and confidential nature thereof and that they agree to and observe the requirements of confidentiality set forth herein; d. not to disclose Information to any other party without the written authorization of Disclosing Party; e. not to duplicate Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party; f. to return Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided the option to either return or destroy information. The changed text removes the 'destroy' option. This creates an inconsistency in how the receiving party should handle the information upon request or termination of the agreement. The contradicted text is the Receiving Party agreeing to return Information which is in written or physical form. The altered text creates a contradiction because the original text allowed for destruction as an alternative, but the changed text and the contradicted text only mention returning.
